questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average age of $30$ students is $12$ years. When the teacher's age is included, the average rises to $13$ years. The teacher's age is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total age = average times number of people.

**Working.** Students' total $=30\times 12=360$; with teacher $=31\times 13=403$. Teacher $=403-360=43$.

**Answer.** $43$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Expressed as a percentage, $0.25$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply a decimal by $100$ to convert to a percentage.

**Working.** $0.25\times 100 = 25\%$.

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All squares are rectangles. A given figure is not a rectangle. Therefore the figure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Contrapositive of 'square → rectangle' is 'not rectangle → not square'.

**Working.** Since it is not a rectangle, it cannot be a square.

**Answer.** is not a square.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct pronoun: 'It was ____ who first reported the gas leak.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** After the linking verb 'was', a subject complement takes the nominative (subject) case.

**Working.** The pronoun renames the subject 'It', so the nominative 'he' is correct (not the objective 'him').

**Answer.** he.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man travels to a town at $40$ km/h and returns along the same road at $60$ km/h. His average speed for the whole journey is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For equal distances, average speed is the harmonic mean.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2\times 40\times 60}{40+60}=\frac{4800}{100}=48$$

**Answer.** $48$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a triangle with base $10$ and height $6$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}\,\text{base}\times\text{height}$.

**Working.** $\tfrac{1}{2}\times 10\times 6 = 30$.

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square-dot pattern grows as $1, 4, 9, \ldots$ (perfect squares). The number of dots in the next figure is _____ .

figure.caption: Square dot arrangements of 1, 4 and 9 dots.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='25' cy='45' r='4' fill='#6366f1'/><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='4'/></g><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='150' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='55' r='4'/></g><text x='215' y='48' font-size='24' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The terms are consecutive perfect squares $n^2$.

**Working.** $1=1^2$, $4=2^2$, $9=3^2$, so the next is $4^2=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A square pyramid of unit cubes is built with a $3\times3$ bottom layer, a $2\times2$ middle layer and a single top cube (figure). Compute the total number of cubes.

figure.caption: Three-layer cube stack
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 210 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='84' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='51' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='73' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='62' width='22' height='22'/></g><text x='28' y='150' font-size='8'>layers: 3x3, 2x2, 1x1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum the cubes in each layer.

**Working.**
$$N=3^2+2^2+1^2=9+4+1=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve the description inside-out.

**Working.** 'The only daughter of my mother' is the woman herself, so 'his mother' is the woman — she is his mother.

**Answer.** Mother.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A $40$-litre mixture contains milk and water in the ratio $3:1$. The volume of water in it is _____ litres.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water fraction $=\dfrac{1}{3+1}$.

**Working.** $40\times\dfrac{1}{4}=10$ litres.

**Answer.** $10$ litres.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Continuous Compounding
questions.stem: For a nominal rate $r$ compounded continuously, the effective annual rate is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuous limit of $(1+r/m)^m$ as $m\to\infty$ is $e^r$.

**Working.** Effective rate $=e^r-1$.

**Answer.** $e^{r}-1$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If the two parallel branches above carry $10$ and $20\,\text{m}^3$/s, the total quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel quantities add.

**Working.**
$$10+20=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A district has $40$ workers, each requiring $0.05\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ of fresh air (figure). Compute the minimum air quantity required ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Per-person air requirement
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='40' width='160' height='40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='120' cy='60' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='60' r='4'/></g><text x='30' y='105' font-size='8'>40 men at 0.05 m^3/s each</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\text{workers}\times\text{rate per worker}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=40\times0.05=2\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $2\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's second (pressure) law, applied to the closed mesh shown in a ventilation network, states that:
options: the algebraic sum of pressure drops around any closed mesh is zero
the sum of quantities entering a junction is always zero
the resistance of any mesh equals the fan pressure
all branches in a mesh carry equal quantity

figure.caption: Closed mesh in a network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,75 110,30 180,75 110,120' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='40' cy='75' r='3'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='180' cy='75' r='3'/><circle cx='110' cy='120' r='3'/></g><text x='62' y='48' font-size='8'>mesh</text><text x='38' y='142' font-size='8'>algebraic sum of drops = 0</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kirchhoff's pressure (mesh) law is an energy-conservation statement.

**Reasoning.** Going once around any closed loop returns to the same pressure, so the algebraic sum of the pressure drops (including any fan pressure rises taken as negative) must be zero.

**Answer.** The algebraic sum of pressure drops around any closed mesh is zero.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A regulator adding $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ of resistance to a split carrying $20\,\text{m}^3$/s imposes an extra pressure drop of _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Extra drop $=R_{\text{reg}}\,Q^2$.

**Working.**
$$0.5\times20^2=0.5\times400=200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The magnetic bearing of a line is 130° and the magnetic declination is 2° East. The true bearing, in degrees, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** True bearing $=$ magnetic bearing $+$ easterly declination.

**Working.**
$$TB=130+2=132^{\circ}$$

**Answer.** 132°.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dump of 1,250,000 m³ loose volume is built to an average height of 25 m. Its base footprint area, in m², is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\dfrac{\text{volume}}{\text{average height}}$.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{1{,}250{,}000}{25}=50{,}000\ \text{m}^2$$

**Answer.** 50,000 m².

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Electronic distance measurement (EDM) determines distance by
options: measuring the travel time or phase difference of modulated electromagnetic waves between instrument and reflector
questions.solution:
**Concept.** EDM uses wave propagation.

**Reasoning.** A modulated light/infrared (or microwave) signal travels to a reflector and back; the phase shift or transit time gives the distance very accurately and quickly.

**Answer.** Phase/time of EM waves.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $0.4\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$, are connected in parallel. Compute the equivalent resistance ($\mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$).

figure.caption: Resistances in parallel
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='65' x2='50' y2='65' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M50 65 L50 35 L190 35 L190 65' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M50 65 L50 95 L190 95 L190 65' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='95' y='23' width='40' height='24' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='95' y='83' width='40' height='24' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='190' y1='65' x2='230' y2='65' stroke='#475569'/><text x='102' y='39' font-size='8'>0.4</text><text x='102' y='99' font-size='8'>0.4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel airways: $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\sum\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_i}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{0.4}}\Rightarrow \sqrt{R_{eq}}=\frac{\sqrt{0.4}}{2}\Rightarrow R_{eq}=\frac{0.4}{4}=0.1$$

**Answer.** $0.1\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The usual logical sequence of mineral exploration is
options: reconnaissance → prospecting → detailed exploration (including drilling) → evaluation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exploration narrows from regional to local.

**Reasoning.** Broad reconnaissance identifies favourable areas; prospecting locates targets; detailed exploration (mapping, geophysics, drilling) and sampling delineate and evaluate the deposit before mining.

**Answer.** Reconnaissance → prospecting → detailed exploration → evaluation.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $4\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$, are connected in parallel (figure). Compute the equivalent resistance ($\mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$).

figure.caption: Resistances in parallel
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='70' x2='50' y2='70' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M50 70 L50 40 L90 40 L90 70' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M50 70 L50 100 L90 100 L90 70' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='90' y='28' width='40' height='24' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='90' y='88' width='40' height='24' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M130 40 L170 40 L170 70' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M130 100 L170 100 L170 70' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='170' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#475569'/><text x='100' y='44' font-size='8'>R=4</text><text x='100' y='104' font-size='8'>R=4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For parallel airways $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_1}}+\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_2}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt{R}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt4}+\frac{1}{\sqrt4}=0.5+0.5=1\implies R=1$$

**Answer.** $1\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An activity finishes earliest at $\text{EF}=10$; its successor starts earliest at $\text{ES}=12$. The free float of the activity is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Free float $=\text{ES(successor)}-\text{EF}$.

**Working.**
$$12-10=2.$$

**Answer.** $2$ days.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A reserve of $2.5\times10^6$ t is mined at an annual production of $500{,}000$ t/yr. Compute the mine life (years).

figure.caption: Reserve and annual output
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='60' height='80' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='110' y1='80' x2='180' y2='80' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ml)'/><defs><marker id='ml' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='35' y='135' font-size='8'>reserve / annual output = life</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mine life $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.**
$$L=\frac{2.5\times10^6}{500000}=5\ \text{years}$$

**Answer.** $5$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: An air sample shows 0.05% CO produced against a 2.5% oxygen deficiency. Graham's ratio $\left(100\times\dfrac{\text{CO}}{\text{O}_2\ \text{deficiency}}\right)$ is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $GR=100\times\dfrac{\text{CO}}{\Delta\text{O}_2}$.

**Working.**
$$GR=100\times\frac{0.05}{2.5}=2.0$$

**Answer.** 2.0 (an elevated value suggesting active heating).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Stokes' theorem equates the surface integral of $(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})$ over $S$ to
options: the line integral of $\mathbf{F}$ around the boundary of $S$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\iint_S(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})\cdot\hat{n}\,dS=\oint_{\partial S}\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r}$.

**Working.** The curl-flux through a surface equals the circulation of $\mathbf{F}$ around its boundary curve.

**Answer.** The line integral of $\mathbf{F}$ around the boundary of $S$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A point source of luminous intensity $200\ \text{cd}$ illuminates a surface $2\ \text{m}$ away, normal to the beam. Compute the illuminance (lux).

figure.caption: Inverse-square illumination
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='14' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'/><g stroke='#f59e0b'><line x1='54' y1='60' x2='190' y2='30'/><line x1='54' y1='60' x2='190' y2='90'/></g><line x1='110' y1='45' x2='110' y2='78' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='80' y='110' font-size='8'>I=200 cd, d=2 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E=\dfrac{I}{d^2}$.

**Working.**
$$E=\frac{200}{2^2}=\frac{200}{4}=50\ \text{lux}$$

**Answer.** $50$ lux.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Converting a future cash amount into its equivalent value today is called
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Present worth divides the future sum by $(1+i)^n$.

**Working.** This process of moving money backward in time is discounting.

**Answer.** discounting.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: For the bench shown with a burden of 3 m, compute the subdrill depth (m) using subdrill = 0.3 × burden.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subdrilling below grade is about 0.3 of the burden to ensure a clean floor.

**Working.**
$$J = 0.3 \times 3 = 0.9$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{0.9}$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At the operating point of the previous fan ($Q=10\,\text{m}^3$/s, $R=2$), the operating pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=RQ^2$.

**Working.**
$$P=2\times10^2=200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Reclamation (restoration) of mined-out opencast land typically involves
options: leaving the spoil heaps untouched permanently
backfilling and regrading, replacing topsoil and revegetating the land for a productive after-use
flooding the entire region with acid water
increasing the depth of the pit indefinitely
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Restore land to a stable, usable condition.

**Reasoning.** Spoil is regraded to stable slopes, topsoil is respread and vegetation/agriculture or forestry re-established, returning the disturbed land to beneficial use and controlling erosion.

**Answer.** Regrade, replace topsoil and revegetate.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) in underground air originates mainly from
options: diesel engine exhaust and blasting fumes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** NOx is a combustion/blasting product.

**Reasoning.** Diesel equipment and detonation of explosives generate oxides of nitrogen; NO₂ is a brown, highly irritant and toxic gas affecting the lungs, requiring good ventilation of headings.

**Answer.** Diesel exhaust and blasting fumes.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Direct (active) attack on a mine fire is appropriate when
options: the fire is small, accessible and can be safely reached and extinguished with water/foam/extinguishers
the fire is large and inaccessible behind goaf
explosive gas has already accumulated heavily
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Method choice depends on accessibility and gas risk.

**Reasoning.** A small, reachable fire with safe gas conditions is best tackled directly using water, foam or extinguishers; large, gassy or inaccessible fires require indirect methods (sealing/inertisation).

**Answer.** Small, accessible fires.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In a Brazilian test, a disc of diameter $50\,\text{mm}$ and thickness $50\,\text{mm}$ fails at $30\,\text{kN}$. The indirect tensile strength $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2P}{\pi D L}$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: A Brazilian disc compressed diametrically between two platens.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='40' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='30' x2='70' y2='110' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='70' y1='10' x2='70' y2='28' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='70,28 64,16 76,16' fill='#334155'/><line x1='70' y1='130' x2='70' y2='112' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='70,112 64,124 76,124' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Brazilian tensile strength $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2P}{\pi D L}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_t=\frac{2(30000)}{\pi(50)(50)}=\frac{60000}{7854}=7.64\,\text{MPa}.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 7.64\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A bit drills 2000 m before replacement. If each hole is 10 m deep, the number of holes drilled per bit is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes per bit $=\dfrac{\text{bit life (m)}}{\text{hole depth}}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{2000}{10}=200$$

**Answer.** 200 holes.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For $\sigma_1=60\,\text{MPa}$ and $\sigma_3=20\,\text{MPa}$, the deviatoric (differential) stress $\sigma_1-\sigma_3$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Differential stress $=\sigma_1-\sigma_3$.

**Working.**
$$60-20=40.$$

**Answer.** $40\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Rankine's method of setting out a circular curve uses
options: offsets from the long chord only
tangential (deflection) angles measured with a theodolite and chord lengths taped on the ground
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deflection angles locate successive chord points.

**Reasoning.** Each chord subtends a tangential angle $\delta=\dfrac{1718.9\,C}{R}$ minutes; the theodolite is set to cumulative deflection angles while the chord length is taped, fixing points on the curve.

**Answer.** Deflection (tangential) angle method.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Natural ventilation in a mine arises from
options: the density difference between the downcast and upcast air columns
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A heavier downcast column displaces the lighter upcast column.

**Working.** The resulting buoyancy drives the airflow.

**Answer.** density difference between downcast and upcast columns.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The cyclone fitted ahead of the filter in a respirable-dust sampler serves to
options: remove the coarse, non-respirable particles so that only the respirable fraction reaches the filter
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Size selection mimics the human airway.

**Reasoning.** The cyclone spins out and discards the larger particles that would be trapped in the upper airways, letting through the fine fraction that penetrates the lungs — so the filter mass reflects the health-relevant respirable dust.

**Answer.** Reject coarse particles; pass the respirable fraction.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The 'true dip' of a bed is the
options: horizontal direction of the bedding plane
maximum angle of inclination of the bedding plane to the horizontal, measured perpendicular to the strike
depth of the bed below surface
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dip measures inclination.

**Reasoning.** True dip is the steepest angle the bed makes with the horizontal, in the direction at right angles to strike; any other direction gives a smaller apparent dip.

**Answer.** Maximum inclination ⟂ strike.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The cumulative-production graph shows a mineable reserve of 10 Mt extracted at a uniform rate of 2 Mt per year. The life of the mine, in years, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='140' x2='220' y2='140' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='140' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='140' x2='190' y2='30' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='220' y2='30' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><text x='140' y='26' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>reserve 10 Mt</text><text x='100' y='158' font-size='9'>years</text><text x='38' y='95' font-size='9' fill='#2563eb'>2 Mt/yr</text></svg>
figure.caption: Cumulative production vs time

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mine life $=\dfrac{\text{mineable reserve}}{\text{annual production rate}}$.

**Working.**
$$L=\frac{10}{2}=5\ \text{years}$$

**Answer.** $5$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers $30\,\text{m}^3$/s at $600\,\text{Pa}$ with an overall efficiency of $75\%$. The motor input power is _____ W.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Input power $=\dfrac{P\,Q}{\eta}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{600\times30}{0.75}=\frac{18000}{0.75}=24000.$$

**Answer.** $24{,}000\,\text{W}=24\,\text{kW}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A walking dragline propels itself by
options: walking shoes/cams, resting on a circular tub while digging
rail bogies on a temporary track
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Draglines are too heavy for crawlers; ground bearing pressure must be very low.

**Reasoning.** The machine rests on a large circular tub (low bearing pressure) while digging, and 'walks' short distances on a pair of eccentric-driven shoes to reposition.

**Answer.** Walking shoes, resting on a tub while digging.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Using $R=\dfrac{kPL}{A^3}$ with $k=0.01\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^4}$, perimeter $P=12\ \text{m}$, length $L=100\ \text{m}$ and area $A=6\ \mathrm{m^2}$ (figure), compute the airway resistance ($\mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$, four decimals).

figure.caption: Airway geometry
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='130' height='55' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='46' y='35' font-size='8'>perimeter 12, A=6</text><text x='46' y='120' font-size='8'>L=100, k=0.01</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Atkinson resistance $R=\dfrac{kPL}{A^3}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\frac{0.01\times12\times100}{6^3}=\frac{12}{216}=0.0556$$

**Answer.** $0.0556\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: An LHD has a 4 m³ bucket at 0.9 fill factor and a cycle time of 90 s. Its loose output, in m³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ bucket $\times$ fill $\times$ cycles/h.

**Working.**
$$\text{cycles/h}=\frac{3600}{90}=40$$
$$Q=4\times0.9\times40=144\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 144 m³/h (loose).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the empirical (68–95–99.7) rule, the approximate fraction of a normal population within one standard deviation of the mean is

figure.caption: Empirical rule: about 68% of a normal distribution lies within one standard deviation of the mean.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='245' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M20 140 C70 140 90 30 132 30 C175 30 195 140 245 140 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='95' y='40' width='74' height='100' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.18)' stroke='none'/><line x1='95' y1='30' x2='95' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='169' y1='30' x2='169' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='110' y='90' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>68%</text><text x='84' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>mu-sigma</text><text x='150' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>mu+sigma</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Empirical rule for the normal distribution.

**Working.** About $68\%$ lies within $\mu\pm\sigma$, $95\%$ within $\mu\pm2\sigma$, $99.7\%$ within $\mu\pm3\sigma$.

**Answer.** $68\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A fire involving energised electrical equipment should NOT be fought with
options: a jet of water (which conducts electricity and risks electrocution)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water conducts electricity.

**Reasoning.** Energised electrical fires must be tackled with non-conducting agents (CO₂, dry powder) after isolating the supply; a water jet risks electrocution and equipment damage.

**Answer.** A water jet.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A skip hoists 10 t per wind and completes one wind every 120 s. The hourly hoisting capacity, in t/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=$ payload $\times$ winds per hour.

**Working.**
$$\text{winds/h}=\frac{3600}{120}=30,\quad Q=10\times30=300\ \text{t/h}$$

**Answer.** 300 t/h.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A pit pump delivers 600 m³/h against a total head of 50 m (water density 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s², efficiency 100%). The hydraulic power, in kW, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Hydraulic power $=\rho g Q H$, with $Q$ in m³/s.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{600}{3600}=0.1667\ \text{m}^3/\text{s}$$
$$P=1000\times9.81\times0.1667\times50=81{,}750\ \text{W}=81.75\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** 81.75 kW.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ each are in parallel, and this group is in series with a $2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ airway. The overall equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Reduce the parallel pair, then add in series.

**Working.**
$$R_{\parallel}=1\ (\text{from }0.5+0.5),\quad R_{eq}=1+2=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A pressure drop of $200\,\text{Pa}$ accompanies a flow of $20\,\text{m}^3$/s. The airway resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R=\dfrac{P}{Q^2}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\frac{200}{400}=0.5.$$

**Answer.** $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The 'angle of draw' in subsidence engineering is
options: the slope angle of the final highwall
the angle from the vertical at the panel edge to the limit of perceptible surface subsidence
the angle of repose of the spoil
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It defines the lateral extent of the subsidence basin.

**Reasoning.** Measured from the vertical at the edge of the extracted panel, the angle of draw locates where surface movement effectively ceases; a larger angle means subsidence extends further beyond the workings.

**Answer.** Vertical-to-limit-of-subsidence angle at the panel edge.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A truck of payload 100 t has a cycle time of 15 min (as shown). Compute its hourly output (t/h).

figure.caption: Haul truck
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='45' width='70' height='30' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='58' cy='80' r='8' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='95' cy='80' r='8' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='48' y='40' font-size='8'>payload 100 t</text><text x='44' y='102' font-size='8'>cycle 15 min</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output = payload × trips per hour, trips/h = 60 ÷ cycle minutes.

**Working.**
$$\frac{60}{15} = 4 \text{ trips/h},\qquad Q = 100 \times 4 = 400$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{400}$ t/h.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y''+y=0$ with $y(0)=1,\;y'(0)=0$, the value of $y\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solution is $y=A\cos t+B\sin t$; apply initial conditions.

**Working.** $y(0)=1\Rightarrow A=1$; $y'(0)=0\Rightarrow B=0$, so $y=\cos t$.
$$y\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{3}\right)=\cos 60^{\circ}=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: On a plane with $c=10\,\text{kPa}$, $\phi=30^\circ$ and normal stress $\sigma_n=40\,\text{kPa}$, the driving shear stress is $30\,\text{kPa}$. The factor of safety is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{c+\sigma_n\tan\phi}{\tau}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_f=10+40\tan 30^\circ=10+23.09=33.09,\quad \text{FoS}=\frac{33.09}{30}=1.10.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.10$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A retarder on a haul truck is used to
options: control and limit truck speed on downgrades without overheating the service brakes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Descending a loaded truck releases large gravitational energy.

**Reasoning.** A retarder (hydraulic/electric/engine) dissipates this energy to hold a safe steady speed on long downgrades, sparing the friction service brakes from overheating.

**Answer.** Controls speed on downgrades.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Three incoherent noise sources each producing $80\ \text{dB}$ act together at a point (figure). Compute the combined sound level (dB, two decimals).

figure.caption: Three equal noise sources
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#475569'><circle cx='60' cy='50' r='12'/><circle cx='100' cy='50' r='12'/><circle cx='140' cy='50' r='12'/></g><circle cx='100' cy='95' r='6' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='32' y='115' font-size='8'>three 80 dB sources at a point</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal sources.

**Working.**
$$L=80+10\log_{10}3=80+4.77=84.77\ \text{dB}$$

**Answer.** $84.77$ dB.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: If the recovery (extraction) factor for the $280{,}000\ \text{t}$ in-situ block is $0.80$, compute the recoverable (mineable) reserve in tonnes.

figure.caption: Recoverable fraction of a reserve
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='40' width='160' height='50' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='40' width='128' height='50' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='40' y='70' font-size='9'>recover 80%</text><text x='30' y='115' font-size='8'>in-situ = 280000 t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recoverable reserve $=$ in-situ reserve $\times$ recovery factor.

**Working.**
$$T_r=280000\times0.80=224000\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $224{,}000$ t.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A longwall face is 200 m long and 2 m high; the shearer takes a 0.8 m web and makes 10 cuts per day. Coal density is 1.4 t/m³. The daily production, in t, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ face length $\times$ web $\times$ height $\times$ density $\times$ cuts/day.

**Working.**
$$Q=200\times0.8\times2\times1.4\times10=4480\ \text{t/day}$$

**Answer.** 4480 t/day.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has $\sigma_c=40\,\text{MPa}$ and tensile strength $\sigma_t=10\,\text{MPa}$. Using $c=\tfrac{1}{2}\sqrt{\sigma_c\sigma_t}$, the cohesion is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $c=\tfrac{1}{2}\sqrt{\sigma_c\sigma_t}$.

**Working.**
$$c=\tfrac{1}{2}\sqrt{40\times10}=\tfrac{1}{2}(20)=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following are INTERPOLATION techniques?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Distinguish interpolation from integration and root-finding.

**Working.**
- Lagrange — interpolation (TRUE).
- Newton's divided difference — interpolation (TRUE).
- Simpson's rule — numerical integration (FALSE).
- Newton–Raphson — root finding (FALSE).

**Answer.** Options (a) and (b).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project costs $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ and returns $\textsf{Rs}\,30{,}000$ per year. The simple payback period is _____ years. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Payback $=\dfrac{\text{investment}}{\text{annual cash flow}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{100000}{30000}=3.33.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 3.33$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At a point the static pressure is $200\,\text{Pa}$ and the velocity pressure is $60\,\text{Pa}$. The total pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ static $+$ velocity pressure.

**Working.**
$$200+60=260.$$

**Answer.** $260\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A friction (Koepe) winder drives the rope by
options: friction over a driving sheave, with a tail rope balancing the head rope; suited to deep shafts
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Koepe principle uses traction, not storage.

**Reasoning.** The rope passes over a friction sheave with conveyances on each side; a tail rope balances the head-rope weight. With no drum to store rope, it suits great depths and shares load across multiple ropes.

**Answer.** Friction over a sheave with a balancing tail rope.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Igneous rocks are termed 'acidic' or 'basic' chiefly on the basis of their
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Silica percentage classifies igneous rocks chemically.

**Reasoning.** Acidic (felsic) rocks such as granite are silica-rich and light; basic (mafic) rocks such as basalt are silica-poorer and dark; ultrabasic rocks have <45% SiO₂.

**Answer.** Silica content.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: At the economic order quantity, the annual
options: ordering cost equals the holding cost
questions.solution:
**Concept.** EOQ balances the two variable costs.

**Working.** $\dfrac{D}{Q}S=\dfrac{Q}{2}H$ at the optimum.

**Answer.** ordering cost equals holding cost.
